coverage_notes: Live MCP: one call made (GitHub Actions run history for refresh-inventory.yml — succeeded). Shopify/Semrush/Vercel/Sentry MCP not attempted per instructions; all other analysis from the 6 staged HTML captures, repo code, and url-inventory/seo-audit staged data. Could NOT check: live sitemap.xml as served (checked app/sitemap.ts + inventory data instead); HTTP status codes / headers of captures (no status metadata in baseline-checks.txt — the 404 capture is assumed to have returned 404, the blog-archive capture 200 based on self-canonical + full render); BlogPosting JSON-LD live output (no live article capture — the article capture 301'd to a PLP — so lib/article-jsonld.ts was verified at code level only; it is complete: headline, image w/ fallback, datePublished, dateModified, author Person/Organization, publisher w/ dimensioned logo, abstract/articleBody/speakable); Semrush mega export is 2026-05-04, predates most fixes, used only for context. PDP Product JSON-LD lacking aggregateRating on the capture is NOT reported as a defect: the code path (lib/product-jsonld.ts:349-364) is correctly guarded and the captured product simply has no product-level reviews; the sitewide Organization aggregateRating (11,423 reviews) is present on every capture. Robots rules, canonical correctness on all 6 captures, heading hierarchy (single H1 on all indexable captures), faceted/pagination handling (?after= cursors noindexed + canonical to base + full A-Z archive for depth-3 discovery), and sitemap lastModified honesty (real updatedAt/publishedAt, no blanket 'now') were all checked and are in good shape — no findings there.

findings.title: Daily URL-inventory refresh workflow has failed 14 days straight — sitemap, redirect table and blog archives frozen at the 2026-06-18 snapshot
findings.evidence: GitHub Actions runs for refresh-inventory.yml (schedule cron '0 4 * * *', .github/workflows/refresh-inventory.yml:39): last success 2026-06-19T09:03Z, then 'completed failure' every day 2026-06-20 through 2026-07-03 (14 consecutive). git log on data/url-inventory/products.json: last snapshot commit cbc8d18 'chore: refresh URL inventory snapshot (#460)' 2026-06-18. app/sitemap.ts:2 builds the entire sitemap from this committed snapshot (lib/inventory), and lib/redirects-table.ts is codegen'd from the same data (next.config.mjs:6-8).
findings.impact: Everything the sitemap advertises — inclusion, lastModified, new products/collections/articles — is 15 days stale. Any product published, retitled, or repriced in Shopify since 6/18 is missing from sitemap.xml; any redirect exported from Shopify Admin since 6/18 is not served at the edge (only hand-edited redirects-manual.json entries work). The workflow even has a failure-notification step (refresh-inventory.yml:170) that evidently isn't reaching anyone.
findings.proposed_fix: Pull the failing run's logs (likely an expired Shopify Admin token or npm/codegen breakage introduced ~6/19), fix the root cause, re-run workflow_dispatch to land a fresh snapshot, and route the existing failure notification to a channel someone reads (or add a repo issue on failure) so a broken snapshot can't silently age for two weeks again.

findings.title: 66 of 218 PDP <title>s render with a mid-string ellipsis — 25 of them cut inside the brand suffix ('· LA Mattres…') — because capTitle's brand-suffix regex doesn't know the '·' separator the PDP uses
findings.evidence: Live capture pdp-standard-foundation-box-spring.html: <title>Standard Profile 9" Box Spring Universal Flat Foundation · LA Mattres…</title>, and identical og:title/twitter:title. Cause: app/(storefront)/products/[handle]/page.tsx:102 appends '· LA Mattress Store' (middle-dot separator) when the resolved title collapses to the H1, then capTitle truncates at 70 chars; lib/seo.ts:34 BRAND_SUFFIX_RE = /\s[|–—-]\s*LA\s+Mattress(?:\s+Store)?\s*$/i matches pipe/dash separators only, so the Phase-292 'drop the whole suffix instead of truncating into it' branch never fires for '·'. Simulation of the exact code path over data/url-inventory/products.json (218 products; 64 have seoTitle identical to title): 66 PDPs emit an ellipsised title, 25 cut mid-suffix (e.g. 'Tempur-Pedic TEMPUR-ProAdapt® Medium Hybrid 12" Mattress · LA Mattres…', 'Diamond Lucille Medium Natural Latex Luxury Euro Top 15" Mattress · L…').
findings.impact: ~30% of the catalog shows a visibly broken title in SERPs and on every social share (og:title inherits it) — exactly the 'broken render' failure mode Phase 292 was built to prevent, hurting CTR on the highest-value commercial pages. The 64 seoTitle==title products all funnel into this branch.
findings.proposed_fix: Add '·' (and any other separators actually used by generators) to BRAND_SUFFIX_RE in lib/seo.ts, or better: make the PDP guard reserve suffix room the way lib/seo.ts ensureTitleDistinctFromH1 already does (capTitle(base, 70 - suffix.length) + suffix) — and switch page.tsx:102 to call ensureTitleDistinctFromH1 instead of its own inline duplicate. Longer-term: backfill distinct seo.title for the 64 products where seoTitle==title.

findings.title: 72 live beds-mattresses articles plus the live blog index are indexable and internally linked but excluded from sitemap.xml — sitemap policy contradicts the /blogs hub policy
findings.evidence: Capture blog-archive-beds-mattresses.html: /blogs/beds-mattresses serves 200, self-canonical (<link rel="canonical" href="https://www.mattressstoreslosangeles.com/blogs/beds-mattresses"/>), no robots meta, 109 unique internal links. app/(storefront)/blogs/page.tsx:26-38 deliberately re-included the blog in the hub after SEMrush 20260628 showed '~72 still render 200'. But app/sitemap.ts:65 DEPRECATED_BLOG_HANDLES = ['beds-mattresses'] with the now-false comment 'every URL under them now 301/308-redirects', and the liveBlogs filter at sitemap.ts:137 drops the index AND all its articles. Recomputed from data/url-inventory/blogs.json + both redirect files: 184 published articles, 112 redirected, 72 live at 200 — none in the sitemap. Meanwhile the automation keeps publishing INTO this blog (data/url-inventory/redirects-manual.json notes of 20260626: 'automation published this article to the DEPRECATED beds-mattresses blog... rendered live (200) but was fully orphaned').
findings.impact: 72 indexable URLs (plus the index) get crawled and indexed via internal links but carry no sitemap lastmod/discovery signal, and Google sees a sitemap that disagrees with the site's own link graph. The stale sitemap.ts comment invites future regressions; the automation-publishes-to-deprecated-blog loop keeps generating orphans that then need manual redirects.
findings.proposed_fix: Pick one policy and encode it once: either (a) keep the blog alive — remove 'beds-mattresses' from DEPRECATED_BLOG_HANDLES and let the existing REDIRECT_SOURCES/isPublished filters (sitemap.ts:188-191, 233-236) naturally emit only the 72 live URLs; or (b) finish the deprecation — 301 the remaining 72 legacy posts (mostly 2010s-era filler like 'george-michael-prince-high-cost-sleep-deprivation') to topical canonicals and re-exclude the blog from the hub. Either way, point the daily-blog automation at sleep-blog/mattress-buying-guide so it stops publishing into the deprecated handle, and refresh the sitemap.ts:50-64 comment. Also worth fixing while in there: the Blog JSON-LD on this index has no @id and no blogPost list (capture shows only name/url/inLanguage/publisher).

findings.title: 404 responses ship a completely empty server-rendered body — zero links, zero headings, content exists only in the RSC flight payload
findings.evidence:
Capture 404-page.html (46KB):
followed only by
findings.impact: Every visitor landing on a dead URL (the site carries 2,029 legacy redirects, so mistyped/expired URLs are common) with JS disabled, blocked, or slow gets a blank white page instead of the recovery grid — lost sessions on exactly the traffic the 404 UX was built to save. Crawlers that read raw HTML see an empty document. noindex is correctly emitted so the pure-SEO cost is contained, but the CSR bailout signals a Suspense/dynamic-API misuse on the not-found path that may affect error boundaries elsewhere.
findings.proposed_fix: Reproduce locally (curl a /pages/<garbage> URL, inspect the HTML body), then find what makes the not-found render bail out of SSR — typically a useSearchParams/dynamic API without a Suspense boundary in the layout chain, or notFound() thrown after the shell flush inside the pages/[handle] Suspense fast-path. Ensure the not-found boundary renders static HTML (both not-found.tsx files are already static-safe; the bailout is in how they're reached).

findings.title: Homepage OpenGraph block is the thinnest on the site: no og:url, og:site_name, og:locale, or og:image dimensions/alt
findings.evidence: homepage.html capture contains exactly three og:* metas (og:title, og:description, og:image → Shopify CDN 'LA_Mattress_Store_-_Logo_Square.png'). Compare the 404 capture (root-layout defaults), which carries og:url, og:site_name, og:locale, og:image:width/height/alt, og:type — the homepage's page-level openGraph override in app/(storefront)/page.tsx replaces rather than extends the root metadata, dropping those fields. Minor sibling nit: homepage canonical is 'https://www.mattressstoreslosangeles.com' (no trailing slash) while every JSON-LD block self-references 'https://www.mattressstoreslosangeles.com/'.
findings.impact: Homepage shares on Slack/WhatsApp/Facebook/X — the single most-shared URL — render from an undersized square logo with no declared dimensions (some scrapers skip images without width/height) and no og:url, weakening share-card consistency. No ranking impact.
findings.proposed_fix: In the homepage metadata, add url, siteName, and a 1200×630 image with width/height/alt to the openGraph object (or reuse the /opengraph-image endpoint the other templates use), and emit the canonical with the trailing slash to match the JSON-LD @id convention.

findings.title: Meta descriptions truncate mid-word ('…at traditional bed h...') — truncDescription cuts at a character, not word, boundary
findings.evidence: pdp-standard-foundation-box-spring.html capture: <meta name="description" content="...Queen from $259 offers rigid flat support at traditional bed h...">. lib/seo.ts:80-83 truncDescription: `s.slice(0, max - 3).trimEnd() + '...'` — no word-boundary or sentence-boundary logic, unlike the article pipeline's truncateToSentence (lib/article-jsonld.ts:97-111) which already solves this.
findings.impact: Any PDP/PLP/page whose source description exceeds 160 chars shows a chopped word + '...' in the SERP snippet — a small polish/CTR cost across the many products that fall back to product.description (usually long).
findings.proposed_fix: Make truncDescription back up to the last space (or reuse truncateToSentence, moved into lib/seo.ts) before appending the ellipsis.
